issues.body: _record_identity deliberately removes GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT, but that attempt changes not only when the failed recorder job alone is retried; it also changes when an operator re-runs all jobs or otherwise re-executes the model work under the same GITHUB_RUN_ID. If that fresh execution reaches the same provider/account/model/folded class, append_record treats it as the old outcome and returns without writing. A second real rate-limit or launch failure can therefore be omitted, weakening consecutive-failure backoff and outage/alert thresholds. The self-test covers only the recorder-only replay interpretation and has no assertion that a genuinely re-executed outcome under the same run ID remains countable.
issues.fix_hint: Use an idempotency identifier that uniquely identifies the immutable produced outcome/recording event rather than collapsing every run attempt, or carry an explicit stable outcome ID that is reused only by recorder retries. Add a non-vacuous test representing a full workflow re-run with the same GITHUB_RUN_ID and a new execution, asserting that its outcome is appended, while a retry of only the recorder remains a no-op.
issues.title: Run-level deduplication drops genuine outcomes from full workflow re-runs

verdict.summary: The jittered CAS retry is bounded and its tests exercise the retry timing and RNG bounds, but the new idempotency key conflates two materially different cases: retrying only the recorder and re-running the actual workflow work. GitHub preserves GITHUB_RUN_ID across both, so a genuine repeated failure can be discarded. Because the ledger drives account backoff and outage alert thresholds, this creates a fail-open undercount and is a major soundness issue.
